L1.area: Block cipher initialization and mode configuration logic

L2.description: A heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability exists where a crafted initialization vector parameter is copied into a fixed-size buffer without proper length validation, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary code.

L3.area: Block cipher constructor and its initialization vector handling for different cipher modes
L3.description: A heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability exists where a crafted initialization vector parameter is copied into a fixed-size buffer without proper length validation when certain cipher modes that do not use an IV are selected, allowing remote attackers to execute arbitrary code.

ground_truth.reason: The ALGnew function in block_template.c copies the user-supplied IV into a fixed-size heap buffer without validating its length when ECB or CTR mode is selected, causing a heap-based buffer overflow.
